message:
fix(test): skip custom panic hook in test mode to prevent kernel poisoning

The runtime_policy tests `runtime_evidence_contract_wrapper_and_cards_export_are_available`,
`runtime_math_log_wrapper_includes_required_traceability_fields`, and
`runtime_kernel_snapshot_wrapper_exposes_live_decision_state` were flaky when run
alongside other module tests.

Root cause: `ensure_minimal_panic_hook()` installs a global panic hook that calls
`mark_kernel_broken()` on ANY panic. When tests from other modules (math_abi, ctype_abi)
triggered assertion failures, the panic hook fired and permanently poisoned the kernel
singleton, causing all subsequent kernel-dependent tests to fail.

Fix: early return from `ensure_minimal_panic_hook()` in test mode (`#[cfg(test)]`).
The test harness owns the panic hook; the custom fallback hook is only needed in the
production cdylib.

Result: 131/131 lib tests pass reliably (verified 5/5 runs, previously 128/131 flaky).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
